articles.title: Canadian Dollar dives on risk-off markets with US CPI on tap
articles.expand: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) accelerates its decline against the US Dollar (USD) on Tuesday, with the USD/CAD pair rallying above 1.3700 and set to test its highest levels in nearly a month, at 1.3714.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Pushes Crude Prices Higher
articles.expand: June WTI crude oil (CLM26 ) on Monday closed up +2.65 (+2.78%), and June RBOB gasoline (RBM26 ) closed up +0.0731 (+2.07%). Crude oil and gasoline prices settled sharply higher on Monday after President Trump rejected Iran's response to his latest peace proposal, which prolongs the closure of the Strait...

articles.title: US CPI set to show prices climbing at fastest pace in nearly three years as Fed rate-cut bets fade
articles.expand: The US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will publish the April Consumer Price Index (CPI) data on Tuesday.

articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Likely extend advance towards 20-day EMA amid firm US Dollar
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair trades 0.26% higher to near 157.60 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The pair gains as the US Dollar (USD) outperforms its peers due to renewed tensions between the United States (US) and Iran.

articles.title: Gold reverses gains as US CPI data takes center stage
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) reverses earlier gains on Tuesday as fading hopes for a near-term breakthrough in US-Iran negotiations and a modest rebound in the US Dollar (USD) weigh on the precious metal ahead of the US Consumer Price Index (CPI) data release.

Brent and WTI are up over $3 and bonds and stocks have been sold. The US dollar is firmer against the G10 currencies but the Norwegian krone. Most emerging market currencies are lower. articles.title: US Dollar: Inflation focus shapes rate expectations – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities economists highlight that higher US Treasury yields followed headlines on Middle East tensions and upcoming supply, with attention now turning to April United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI).

articles.title: US Dollar: Hotter CPI and equity risks – ING

articles.title: Japanese Yen underperforms vs USD as Iran tensions offset intervention risks before US CPI
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair reverses a knee-jerk decline to the 156.75-156.70 region and climbs to the top end of its daily range during the first half of the European session.

articles.title: ECB: June hike call challenged by data path – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities economists diverge from consensus by expecting the European Central Bank (ECB) to hold rates in June. They stress the absence of clear second-round effects, anchored inflation expectations, and softening confidence and growth.

articles.title: Dow Jones futures remains flat ahead of US CPI inflation data
articles.expand: Dow Jones futures are steady around 49,800 during the European hours on Tuesday, ahead of the United States (US) regular opening. Meanwhile, the S P 500 decline 0.35% to near 7,410, and the Nasdaq 100 futures inch lower 0.77% toward 29,200.

articles.title: Australian Dollar: Budget tightening focus – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank's Senior Market Strategist Benjamin Picton highlights comments from Australian Prime Minister Albanese and Treasurer Chalmers ahead of the Commonwealth budget, stressing concerns over an expensive housing market and planned tax changes.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Languishes near 0.7200 as USD sticks to gains ahead of US CPI
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair continues with its struggle to find acceptance above the 0.7250 level and attracts fresh sellers on Tuesday as rising US-Iran tensions boost the safe-haven US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: UK consumer spending falls for first time since 2024 as Iran war bites

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.
